name: Digging Up Trouble
description: From Starfleet's first contact with a Q, they have been understood as a godlike species existing beyond the bounds of spacetime. But the idea of a Q homeworld presents other possibilities. What answers could lie in wait there? What dangers?
rules:
-- In a Skirmish Event, you will engage in space battles against thematically appropriate foes until the Event's conclusion.
-- There are two Phases to this Event! Each Phase is individually timed.
-- Unique Ship Battle Missions are available during the event. Open the Event to access them.
-- Ships of any rarity can be used at all difficulty levels.
-- Elite difficulty opponents are upgraded to Epic difficulty level, Elite difficulty VP rewards are upgraded to Epic difficulty VP rewards.
-- Epic difficulty opponent stats are 20% higher, Epic difficulty VP rewards are doubled.
-- Using Wingman Q, any version of Petra, Korby, Mariner, Exoarchaeologists, Q Continuum crew, or crew matching a Battle Trait will provide a bonus.
-- Using the Free Spirit, the I.S.S. Cerritos, or the U.S.S. Defiant will provide a bonus to hull.
-- Every Ship Battle you win earns you Event Victory Points. Collect Event Victory Points to unlock Threshold Rewards!
-- In this event, Squadrons may also compete to advance up the Squadron leaderboards. Join a Squadron to get more rewards!
bonus_text: Crew Bonus: Wingman Q, Petra, Korby, Mariner, Exoarchaeologists, Q Continuum

featured_crew.name: Q
featured_crew.flavor: Any article, no matter how provocative, needs good editing. Even if it's Quinn's article.

featured_crew.name: Korby
featured_crew.flavor: On Planet Vadia IX, Korby and Chapel found proof the M'Kroon descended from an ancient, galaxy-spanning civilization.

action.name: Theoretical Doesn't Mean You Can Just Go And Walk On Air

featured_crew.name: Vindicta
featured_crew.flavor: Captain of the Vinpocalypse, marauder Vindicta is determined to take down Captain Freeman at any cost--preferably on a rickety catwalk.

rewards.name: Credits
rewards.flavor: Credits are the galaxy's standard currency. Use Credits in the Time Portal to enlist crew members, build items, and more.

rewards.name: Mariner
rewards.flavor: Beckett Mariner is a command ensign on the USS Cerritos. She breaks dumb rules that shouldn’t be there so she can do a better job!

rewards.name: Training
rewards.flavor: Increases crew XP by 2500.

rewards.name: Chronitons
rewards.flavor: Chronitons allow your crew to take action in these chaotic times. Use Chronitons to replay parts of past missions via the Timeline.

ship.name: U.S.S. Defiant
ship.flavor: Initially created for use against the Borg. Equipped with ablative armor and Starfleet's first legal cloaking device.

rewards.flavor: Use to instantly revive a failed Voyage.

rewards.flavor: Contains ONE of the following 5 Polestars: Common, Uncommon, Rare, Super rare, Legendary

rewards.name: Skill
rewards.flavor: Contains ONE of the following 6 Polestars: Command, Diplomacy, Security, Engineering, Science, Medicine

rewards.name: ISM
rewards.flavor: ISM, or interstellar medium, is the matter that exists between astronomical objects. It can be manipulated by the Q to facilitate crew retrieval. ISM can be obtained from Daily Log-in Rewards, Campaigns and participating in Fleet activities.

rewards.flavor: Contains ONE of the following 6 Polestars: Command, Diplomacy, Security, Engineering, Science, Medicine

rewards.name: ISM
rewards.flavor: ISM, or interstellar medium, is the matter that exists between astronomical objects. It can be manipulated by the Q to facilitate crew retrieval. ISM can be obtained from Daily Log-in Rewards, Campaigns and participating in Fleet activities.

rewards.name: Dilithium
rewards.flavor: Dilithium is your most valuable asset. Use Dilithium in the Time Portal to acquire valuable crew members, items, and more.

rewards.name: Honor
rewards.flavor: Honor is gained by dismissing crew who have served you well, but are ready for reassignment. Redeem in the Time Portal for exclusive crew, special fusion-related items, training programs, and more.

rewards.name: Merits
rewards.flavor: Merits can be earned by completing missions. Use Merits to acquire faction-based rewards, upgrades to your ship, and more.

rewards.name: Training
rewards.flavor: Increases crew XP by 2500.

rewards.name: Q
rewards.flavor: Any article, no matter how provocative, needs good editing. Even if it's Quinn's article.

rewards.name: Vindicta
rewards.flavor: Captain of the Vinpocalypse, marauder Vindicta is determined to take down Captain Freeman at any cost--preferably on a rickety catwalk.

content.reroll_info:
Skirmish rewards are additive. By continuously rolling you receive additional rewards. No rewards are lost by rolling again.
DROP RATES:
Event VP: 13.58%
Time Portal Pack: 0.13%
Chronitons: 11.45%
Honor: 14.11%
Intel: 27.43%
Credits: 33.29%

Skirmish rewards are additive. By continuously rolling you receive additional rewards. No rewards are lost by rolling again.

Event VP: 13.58%
Time Portal Pack: 0.13%
Chronitons: 11.45%
Honor: 14.11%
Intel: 27.43%
Credits: 33.29%

screens.text: "Come on, Captain, let's go! Ships are heading to Vadia IX to dig for dirt on Q and his wannabe god buddies. Have you heard the rumors going around? They actually had a homeworld? If that's true. Q has no excuse to be as insufferable as he is, lording his immortality over us!"
"However our species did or didn't start, we still have centuries of wisdom, light years of explored space, and powers your mortal minds could only imagine. But go on, dig in the dirt like dogs."
"You humans think yourselves so removed from the canines you domesticate when in reality, you're much closer to them intellectually and evolutionarily than you'll ever be to us."

"All this rambling about wisdom and intelligence - didn't your Daddy scold you for your foolish behavior before? We have a right to know your origins, especially if you originated from the same homeworld sealing away intergalactic horrors. I mean, what does that say about you Q?"

"Typical human arrogance. You think yourselves so clever, that with enough time and effort, the universe will grant any answer you seek. Or perhaps you'll just make one up one. Yet the universe does not function so neatly. Go seek your answers on Vadia IX - if you can reach it!"

"Come on, Captain, let's go! Ships are heading to Vadia IX to dig for dirt on Q and his wannabe god buddies. Have you heard the rumors going around? They actually had a homeworld? If that's true. Q has no excuse to be as insufferable as he is, lording his immortality over us!"
"However our species did or didn't start, we still have centuries of wisdom, light years of explored space, and powers your mortal minds could only imagine. But go on, dig in the dirt like dogs."
"You humans think yourselves so removed from the canines you domesticate when in reality, you're much closer to them intellectually and evolutionarily than you'll ever be to us."

"All this rambling about wisdom and intelligence - didn't your Daddy scold you for your foolish behavior before? We have a right to know your origins, especially if you originated from the same homeworld sealing away intergalactic horrors. I mean, what does that say about you Q?"

"Typical human arrogance. You think yourselves so clever, that with enough time and effort, the universe will grant any answer you seek. Or perhaps you'll just make one up one. Yet the universe does not function so neatly. Go seek your answers on Vadia IX - if you can reach it!"
